_meta.purpose: WIDA proficiency-level adaptations for bilingual teaching strategies
_meta.description: Proficiency-responsive implementations, scaffolds, and language objectives for bilingual strategies aligned with WIDA 2020 framework
_meta.target_use: Integration with lesson plan transformation prompt for precise multilingual learner support

grade_cluster_focus.4-5: Strategic L1 use for academic concept development
grade_cluster_focus.9-12: Advanced bilingual discourse for specialized content

levels_1_2.implementation: Heavy L1 use for concept understanding, English for key vocabulary
levels_1_2.language_objective_template: Students will use Portuguese to understand [content] and express key ideas using English words: [vocabulary list]
levels_1_2.assessment: L1 explanation with English vocabulary identification

levels_3_4.implementation: Strategic L1 for complex ideas, increasing English sentence production
levels_3_4.language_objective_template: Students will explain [content] using Portuguese for complex ideas and English sentences with frames: [specific frames]
levels_3_4.assessment: Mixed-language explanations with English sentence structures

levels_5_6.implementation: Sophisticated translanguaging for nuanced academic discourse
levels_5_6.language_objective_template: Students will demonstrate [content understanding] through strategic translanguaging, producing [product] with academic English discourse features
levels_5_6.assessment: Academic discourse with purposeful language choices

common_pitfalls: Overuse of L1 preventing English development
success_indicators: Purposeful language choices for different audiences
Increased academic vocabulary in both languages

grade_cluster_focus.4-5: Academic language development with content mastery
grade_cluster_focus.6-8: Complex academic discourse in content areas
grade_cluster_focus.9-12: Specialized academic language for college readiness

levels_1_2.implementation: Heavy visual supports, hands-on activities, minimal text
levels_1_2.language_objective_template: Students will identify [content vocabulary] using visuals and demonstrate understanding through [concrete action]
levels_1_2.assessment: Non-verbal demonstrations, picture identification

levels_3_4.implementation: Graphic organizers, sentence frames, structured academic talk
levels_3_4.language_objective_template: Students will explain [content concept] using [specific frames] and produce [structured output]
levels_3_4.assessment: Structured responses using academic language frames

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex academic discourse, independent research, critical analysis
levels_5_6.language_objective_template: Students will analyze [content] using academic discourse features and produce [complex product] demonstrating [specific criteria]
levels_5_6.assessment: Academic writing, formal presentations with specialized vocabulary

common_pitfalls: Lowering content expectations instead of providing language support
success_indicators: Independent use of academic language structures

grade_cluster_focus.4-5: Complex reasoning shared through organized discourse

levels_1_2.implementation: Think with visuals/L1, pair with bilingual partner, share through gestures/single words
levels_1_2.language_objective_template: Students will think about [topic] using L1/visuals, discuss with partner, and share one idea using [English word/phrase]

timing_adaptations.share: Single words or phrases with gestures

levels_1_2.assessment: Participation observation, gesture/visual responses

levels_3_4.implementation: Think in L1/L2, pair with structured frames, share complete sentences
levels_3_4.language_objective_template: Students will discuss [topic] using [specific frames] and share responses in complete sentences with [target vocabulary]
levels_3_4.assessment: Structured response rubric, academic vocabulary usage

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex reasoning, academic discussion, sophisticated sharing
levels_5_6.language_objective_template: Students will analyze [topic] through discussion, using academic discourse features to share [complex reasoning] with evidence
levels_5_6.assessment: Academic discourse rubric, evidence-based reasoning

common_pitfalls: Not allowing sufficient think time for language processing
Pairing students without considering language compatibility
success_indicators: Increased participation from all proficiency levels

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture-based organizers with minimal text, L1 labels allowed
levels_1_2.language_objective_template: Students will organize [content] using pictures and key English words: [vocabulary list]
levels_1_2.assessment: Picture placement accuracy, key vocabulary identification

levels_3_4.implementation: Text and visual combinations with sentence frames
levels_3_4.language_objective_template: Students will organize [content] using [specific organizer] and complete sentences with [target structures]
levels_3_4.assessment: Complete sentence usage, academic vocabulary integration

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex academic organizers for analysis and synthesis
levels_5_6.language_objective_template: Students will analyze [content] using [complex organizer] to demonstrate [academic skill] with specialized vocabulary
levels_5_6.assessment: Academic analysis quality, sophisticated language use

common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on organizers without language development
Using organizers too complex for proficiency level
Not connecting organizer content to oral/written production
success_indicators: Transfer of organizational patterns to writing
Increased academic vocabulary through visual organization

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture dictionaries with L1-L2 visual pairs, teacher modeling of dictionary use with think-alouds
levels_1_2.assessment: Picture identification, gesture responses to vocabulary

levels_3_4.implementation: Bilingual dictionaries with context practice, student-created vocabulary cards with definitions
levels_3_4.assessment: Vocabulary usage in sentences, definition explanations

levels_5_6.implementation: Strategic dictionary use for academic precision, etymology exploration, register awareness
levels_5_6.assessment: Sophisticated vocabulary use, register appropriateness

grade_cluster_considerations.K-1: Focus on high-frequency concrete words with visual support
grade_cluster_considerations.2-3: Academic vocabulary introduction with context clues
grade_cluster_considerations.4-5: Complex academic terms with morphological analysis
grade_cluster_considerations.9-12: Sophisticated academic register and nuanced meanings

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture-heavy word cards with L1-L2 pairs, color coding for categories, interactive pointing games
levels_1_2.assessment: Word recognition, category sorting, basic usage

levels_3_4.implementation: Interactive word wall games, student-created cards, morphology highlighting, sentence frame integration
levels_3_4.assessment: Sentence creation, word relationship explanations

levels_5_6.implementation: Student-led vocabulary instruction, etymology displays, academic register distinctions, cross-curricular connections
levels_5_6.assessment: Vocabulary instruction leadership, complex usage demonstrations

grade_cluster_considerations.K-1: Basic nouns and verbs with pictures, alphabet organization

levels_1_2.implementation: 3-5 key words with visual supports, L1 cognate connections, TPR integration, repeated exposure
levels_1_2.assessment: Word recognition, simple matching, gesture responses

levels_3_4.implementation: Academic vocabulary pre-teaching with graphic organizers, word analysis, context practice
levels_3_4.assessment: Definition provision, contextual usage, explanation tasks

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex vocabulary instruction with nuanced meanings, register awareness, etymology connections
levels_5_6.assessment: Sophisticated usage, register appropriateness, complex explanations

levels_1_2.implementation: Visual cognate matching, side-by-side L1-L2 displays, pronunciation practice
levels_1_2.assessment: Cognate identification, pattern recognition

levels_3_4.implementation: Cognate hunting activities, false cognate awareness, morphological pattern recognition
levels_3_4.assessment: Cognate discovery, accurate usage, pattern application

levels_5_6.implementation: Etymology analysis, register variations of cognates, academic cognate networks
levels_5_6.assessment: Etymology explanations, register distinctions, network creation

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture-rich texts, frequent pausing for comprehension, L1 discussion time, gesture responses
levels_1_2.assessment: Gesture responses, picture identification, L1 comprehension checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Strategic stopping points, sentence frames for responses, partner discussions, vocabulary pre-teaching
levels_3_4.assessment: Prediction accuracy, summary completion, question formation

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex text analysis, academic discourse modeling, critical thinking questions, independent responses
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex analysis, academic discourse, independent critical responses

levels_1_2.implementation: Structured partner work with visual supports, L1 discussion encouraged, simple role assignments
levels_1_2.assessment: Partner participation, simple idea sharing

levels_3_4.implementation: Small group protocols, sentence frames for discussion, rotating leadership roles
levels_3_4.assessment: Protocol following, academic discussion, leadership demonstration

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex collaborative tasks, academic argumentation, peer teaching opportunities
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex collaboration, academic argumentation, peer teaching

levels_1_2.implementation: Non-verbal response options, picture-based assessments, L1 explanation allowed
levels_1_2.assessment: Non-verbal demonstration, picture identification, L1 explanation

levels_3_4.implementation: Multiple assessment modalities, sentence frame supports, peer assessment opportunities
levels_3_4.assessment: Multi-modal demonstration, frame usage, peer assessment

levels_5_6.implementation: Self-assessment tools, academic language focus, complex reasoning demonstration
levels_5_6.assessment: Self-assessment accuracy, academic language use, complex reasoning

levels_1_2.implementation: Cultural artifact sharing, family photo discussions, simple cultural comparisons with L1 support
levels_1_2.assessment: Cultural sharing, family descriptions, basic comparisons

levels_3_4.implementation: Structured cultural presentations, heritage language connections, community knowledge integration
levels_3_4.assessment: Cultural presentations, heritage connections, knowledge sharing

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex cultural analysis, critical cultural discussions, academic cultural research
levels_5_6.assessment: Cultural analysis, critical discussions, academic research

levels_1_2.implementation: Family skill sharing with visual supports, simple community connections, L1 storytelling
levels_1_2.assessment: Show-and-tell, labeled visuals, brief L1 explanations

levels_3_4.implementation: Structured interviews with family/community, bilingual note-taking, short presentations
levels_3_4.assessment: Interview summaries, framed presentations, connection statements

levels_5_6.implementation: Community knowledge research projects, comparative analysis, academic presentations
levels_5_6.assessment: Analytical write-ups, formal presentations, synthesis products

levels_1_2.implementation: Shared experiences with L1 oral recounts; teacher scribes into English with visuals
levels_1_2.assessment: Picture-sequencing, labeled sentences, oral retell

levels_3_4.implementation: Bilingual drafting from shared text; guided revisions using frames
levels_3_4.assessment: Paragraph drafts with frames, oral summaries

levels_5_6.implementation: Independent LEA cycles with academic language focus and audience awareness
levels_5_6.assessment: Well-organized narratives/explanations with academic features

levels_1_2.implementation: Provide highly supported stems with visuals and word banks for short responses
levels_1_2.assessment: Oral/written completion of stems with key vocabulary

levels_3_4.implementation: Offer function-specific frames (compare, explain) and partner rehearsal
levels_3_4.assessment: Complete sentences using target structures in responses

levels_5_6.implementation: Transition from frames to discourse markers; model register choices
levels_5_6.assessment: Extended responses demonstrating accurate academic structures

levels_1_2.implementation: Teacher models actions; students respond physically before producing language
levels_1_2.assessment: Accurate physical responses; picture-to-command matching

levels_3_4.implementation: Students give simple commands and narrate short action sequences
levels_3_4.assessment: Oral narration of sequences; paired command accuracy

levels_5_6.implementation: Students design multi-step procedures and teach peers using precise language
levels_5_6.assessment: Peer-taught mini-lessons; rubrics for clarity and precision

levels_1_2.implementation: Dual objectives with visuals, L1 preview/review, structured interaction
levels_1_2.assessment: Picture-based checks, organizer completion

levels_3_4.implementation: Guided academic talk with frames; integrated reading/writing tasks
levels_3_4.assessment: Structured explanations and short writing with frames

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex discourse with discipline-specific language; independent application
levels_5_6.assessment: Extended writing/presentations with disciplinary features

levels_1_2.implementation: Model metacognitive and cognitive strategies with visuals and L1 mediation
levels_1_2.assessment: Checklist use, strategy naming with visuals

levels_3_4.implementation: Guided practice using summarizing, inferring, questioning with frames
levels_3_4.assessment: Reflections citing strategies; framed explanations

levels_5_6.implementation: Independent selection and evaluation of strategies; academic reflection
levels_5_6.assessment: Analytical reflections connecting strategy use to outcomes

levels_1_2.implementation: Flexible grouping with L1 supports and visual materials; simplified outputs
levels_1_2.assessment: Visual products, labeled work, simple performances

levels_3_4.implementation: Tiered tasks with sentence frames and organizer supports; choice of outputs
levels_3_4.assessment: Tiered rubrics; structured responses with academic vocabulary

levels_5_6.implementation: Open-ended tasks with academic discourse expectations and peer feedback
levels_5_6.assessment: Extended products with discipline-specific criteria

levels_1_2.implementation: Entry events with visuals/L1; scaffolded roles and simple products
levels_1_2.assessment: Structured check-ins; product checklists with visuals

levels_3_4.implementation: Research with guided notes; intermediate presentations with frames
levels_3_4.assessment: Milestone presentations; rubrics highlighting language targets

levels_5_6.implementation: Authentic audience products; academic discourse and revision cycles
levels_5_6.assessment: Final presentations/reports assessed for content and language

levels_1_2.implementation: I-do modeling with visuals; high support during guided practice
levels_1_2.assessment: Guided practice demonstrations; simple exit checks

levels_3_4.implementation: We-do with sentence frames; gradual release to structured independence
levels_3_4.assessment: Independent practice artifacts with light scaffolds

levels_5_6.implementation: You-do with accountable talk and metacognitive reflection
levels_5_6.assessment: Performance tasks with academic criteria

levels_1_2.implementation: Two-column charts mapping L1 to English with visuals
levels_1_2.assessment: Accurate mappings; picture-word checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Cognate/false cognate comparisons and sentence-level transfer
levels_3_4.assessment: Short English summaries using transferred vocabulary

levels_5_6.implementation: Conceptual transfer to academic writing/presentations with register awareness
levels_5_6.assessment: Products demonstrating accurate cross-linguistic transfer

levels_1_2.implementation: Brief L1 clarification followed by immediate English restatement
levels_1_2.assessment: Observation of brevity and accurate English restatement

levels_3_4.implementation: Planned L1 use for complex ideas; logs to monitor frequency
levels_3_4.assessment: Student ability to bridge back to English accurately

levels_5_6.implementation: Purposeful L1 use during academic discourse with register alignment
levels_5_6.assessment: Academic talk demonstrating strategic language choices

levels_1_2.implementation: Brief L1 preview of key ideas and vocabulary; English instruction; L1 review
levels_1_2.assessment: Comprehension checks at each phase

levels_3_4.implementation: Targeted L1 concept scaffolds; English practice; L1 synthesis
levels_3_4.assessment: English responses with L1-supported accuracy

levels_5_6.implementation: Selective L1 support for nuance; independent English products
levels_5_6.assessment: Products demonstrating conceptual mastery and precise language

levels_1_2.implementation: Read L1 version first; picture walk; key vocab bridges
levels_1_2.assessment: Retell with visuals; vocab checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Compare translation choices and structures; short English summaries
levels_3_4.assessment: Compare/contrast responses; summaries

levels_5_6.implementation: Critical analysis of translation/register; academic presentations
levels_5_6.assessment: Analytical responses and presentations

levels_1_2.implementation: Identify positive transfer (pictures, cognates) and apply in simple tasks
levels_1_2.assessment: Evidence of transfer in simple outputs

levels_3_4.implementation: Teach explicit transfer strategies across reading/writing tasks
levels_3_4.assessment: Reflections and products showing transfer use

levels_5_6.implementation: Strategic planning for transfer in complex academic products
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex products with explicit transfer evidence

levels_1_2.implementation: Notice simple patterns (cognates, plurals) and discuss in L1/English
levels_1_2.assessment: Pattern identification tasks; oral explanations

levels_3_4.implementation: Compare forms/structures across languages; apply to editing
levels_3_4.assessment: Edited drafts showing informed choices

levels_5_6.implementation: Analyze register and nuance across languages; justify choices
levels_5_6.assessment: Analytical explanations and refined products

levels_1_2.implementation: Share heritage language words/stories; connect to classroom concepts
levels_1_2.assessment: Short shares with visual supports

levels_3_4.implementation: Explore cultural/linguistic practices and connect to content through mini-projects
levels_3_4.assessment: Mini-project products and presentations

levels_5_6.implementation: Conduct inquiries linking heritage practices to academic topics; present findings
levels_5_6.assessment: Formal presentations/writing with academic features

levels_1_2.implementation: Collect multimodal work (drawings, labels, L1 notes) over time
levels_1_2.assessment: Growth evidence across samples; simple reflections

levels_3_4.implementation: Include bilingual reflections and goal-setting; rubric-aligned artifacts
levels_3_4.assessment: Periodic conferences; criterion-referenced reviews

levels_5_6.implementation: Curate discipline-specific products with academic commentary
levels_5_6.assessment: Portfolio defenses/presentations

levels_1_2.implementation: Teach feedback stems; allow L1 talk before English feedback
levels_1_2.assessment: Use of frames; respectful, task-focused feedback

levels_3_4.implementation: Guided peer reviews with criteria; sentence-framed feedback
levels_3_4.assessment: Quality/accuracy of feedback against criteria

levels_5_6.implementation: Student-led critiques with academic register and evidence
levels_5_6.assessment: Evidence-based, register-appropriate feedback

levels_1_2.implementation: Performance-based tasks with visuals and demonstrations; L1 allowed for content
levels_1_2.assessment: Task performance with minimal English load

levels_3_4.implementation: Choice boards with scaffolded language demands; guided oral/written outputs
levels_3_4.assessment: Rubrics separating content vs language criteria

levels_5_6.implementation: Authentic products (models, exhibits) with academic language expectations
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex products evaluated for content mastery and language use

levels_1_2.implementation: Assess simple functions with visuals and frames; separate content vs language
levels_1_2.assessment: Quick checks targeting specific functions

levels_3_4.implementation: Rubrics for language functions (explain, compare) within content tasks
levels_3_4.assessment: Documented growth on targeted functions

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex function assessment (argue, synthesize) with academic criteria
levels_5_6.assessment: Extended responses evaluated with function-focused rubrics

levels_1_2.implementation: Same-L1 pairing for comprehension; English rehearsal for output
levels_1_2.assessment: Checklists for tutoring moves; simple oral checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Structured tutoring scripts with frames and goal tracking
levels_3_4.assessment: Goal attainment evidence; tutor logs

levels_5_6.implementation: Content-specific tutoring with academic discourse and metacognitive prompts
levels_5_6.assessment: Tutor/tutee reflections; observed academic discourse

levels_1_2.implementation: Present side-by-side L1–English examples with visuals; highlight simple patterns and exceptions.
levels_1_2.assessment: Sort/match activities and oral identification of similarities/differences

levels_3_4.implementation: Compare grammar/phonology patterns and transform short sentences across languages using frames.
levels_3_4.assessment: Accurate transformed sentences and brief explanations using frames

levels_5_6.implementation: Analyze register differences and non-equivalences; apply insights to editing and revision.
levels_5_6.assessment: Edited drafts with rationale referencing cross-linguistic analysis

1.motor_skills: Developing fine motor, simple writing tasks

selection_criteria: Match strategy to student proficiency levels (not grade level alone)
Align with lesson's Key Language Use (Narrate/Inform/Explain/Argue)
Target appropriate language dimension (Word/Phrase → Sentence → Discourse)
Ensure cultural and linguistic asset integration

scaffolding_progression.levels_3_4: Guided practice → Independent practice with support

global_defaults.language_objective_template: Students will use language to [KEY_LANGUAGE_USE] [content_focus] (ELD-[standard].[grade_cluster].[KLU].[mode]) by [language_function] using [supports] appropriate for WIDA levels [range], producing [product] that demonstrates [dimension_focus].

by_key_language_use.Narrate: I will tell about [content_focus] using [sequence words] and a [visual].
by_key_language_use.Inform: I will describe [content_focus] using a [word bank] and a [chart/diagram].
by_key_language_use.Explain: I will explain [content_focus] using [frames/organizer] and evidence.

by_dimension.word_phrase: I will label [terms] using a word bank on a diagram.
by_dimension.sentence: I will describe [topic] using sentence frames in 2–3 sentences.
by_dimension.discourse: I will explain [idea] using an organizer in a short paragraph.

word_phrase: Teaching long word lists without context
No practice applying words in context
sentence: Frames not aligned to target function
No oral rehearsal prior to writing
discourse: Lack of explicit transitions and cohesion
Products too linguistically demanding for proficiency levels

word_phrase: Accurate use of target vocabulary in context
Recognition and control of key morphology
sentence: Use of connectors for cause/effect, compare/contrast
